evidence: src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts combines normalization, logging, UI notification dispatch, global notifier registration, and mutable module-level singleton state (`registeredNotifier`, `removeNotifierListener`).

Feature logic depends on shared infra hubs directly: src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx and src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts both call `handleError(...)` for non-fatal/control-flow branches, increasing central coupling.

src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx mixes primitive wrapper exports (`Tooltip`, `TooltipProvider`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent`) with feature-level touch behavior (`TouchableTooltip`) and document-level pointer listeners.

src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/scenarios.ts imports `InstallVisualKey` from src/pages/Home/components/installInstructions/visuals.tsx, coupling decision logic to a rendering-heavy module.

Gallery orchestration is split across pass-through context bags in src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryController.ts and src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts, obscuring ownership boundaries.

Orchestration identity is read via multiple fallback paths in completion queries (`params->>orchestrator_run_id`, `params->>run_id`, `params->orchestrator_details->>run_id`; similarly for orchestrator task id) in `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:191` and `:208`, indicating schema drift at the module boundary.

Task payload builders write overlapping orchestration keys (`orchestrator_task_id`, `run_id`, nested `orchestration_contract`) in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:122-130` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:292-320`, increasing integration seam ambiguity.

Video lightbox composition has bidirectional coupling between component and render-model hook (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx` imports `useVideoLightboxRenderModel`, while `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:8-12` imports types from `../VideoLightbox`), making seam ownership less clear.

Shared infra dependency creation is hidden at module scope in multiple hooks (`const supabase = getSupabaseClient()` in `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:13`, `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:27`, `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts:5`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:13`), reducing boundary testability and explicit handoff control.

Legacy migration surfaces are documented with sunset comments but remain open-ended in code: `LegacyProjectImageSettings` keeps deprecated keys and is merged back into active input types (`legacyProjectImageSettings.ts:10-13`, `:15-31`, `:38-40`).

Multiple compatibility/deprecation paths are still live in internals (`storage.ts:217-219` deprecated alias export, `taskIdHelpers.ts:27-32` legacy key fallback chain), increasing branchiness and retention of obsolete code paths.

Several helpers rely on brittle string heuristics and ad-hoc guards instead of explicit typed predicates (`preloader.ts:41-44` filename substring skip, `imageAspectRatio.ts:57-59` narrow resolution parsing).

Hook internals include wrapper/cast patterns that obscure intent and weaken directness (`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:48-58` component factory in hook state path, `:64` forced cast of mutation variables).

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryController.ts composes and re-nests context bags, then mostly forwards values to useMediaGalleryHandlers/useMobileInteractions with minimal new behavior.
src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts has four near-identical build*Props helpers that mostly copy the same shared fields into different objects.

src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts introduces multiple orchestration wrappers (environment/persistence/inpainting/reposition/img2img/settings sync) that primarily shuttle data between hooks.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useButtonGroupProps.ts uses `useMemo(..., [params])` while callers typically create fresh `params` objects, so memoization does not prevent recompute and still adds abstraction layers.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx builds many intermediate memoized objects (`lightboxCore`, `lightboxMedia`, `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxNavigation`) before constructing state, increasing navigation overhead for changes.

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx and src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryController.ts pass large nested prop/context objects across hook boundaries, creating wrapper-chain churn.

src/shared/lib/tasks/orchestrationContract.ts returns `Record<string, unknown>` and accepts unconstrained `taskFamily: string`, so the API does not expose a concrete contract despite being named as one.

supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts must query multiple key aliases (`orchestrator_run_id`, `run_id`, nested `orchestrator_details.run_id`; similarly for orchestrator task id), indicating producer/consumer contract drift.

src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts strips legacy keys and re-emits orchestration fields in multiple places (`orchestrator_details`, top-level params, and `orchestration_contract`), which obscures the canonical interface.
src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts, src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts, and src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts catch, call `handleError`, then rethrow.
src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts catches and calls `handleError` but suppresses errors and does not rethrow/return a failure signal.

src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts handles some missing-state conditions via `handleError` and early return, while other paths attempt operations inside try/catch; caller-visible failure behavior is mixed.

findings.summary: Global error handler acts as a cross-cutting hub with mixed responsibilities and runtime channel wiring.
evidence: handleError.ts owns normalization/logging plus browser event emission and notifier lifecycle via module globals.
useMobile.tsx routes device-detection exceptions into handleError, binding a low-level environment hook to the app-wide error pipeline.
useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts calls handleError for local shot/media workflow misses, making feature control paths depend on the same global error hub.
findings.suggestion: Split error concerns into separate modules: pure normalization/logging core, optional UI notifier adapter, and feature-local guards. Keep shared hooks on a minimal reporting interface to reduce cross-subsystem coupling.

findings.summary: Shared tooltip primitive file includes product-level touch interaction logic, blurring ownership boundaries.
evidence: tooltip.tsx exports both primitive wrappers and `TouchableTooltip` with mobile toggling, pointerdown outside handling, and cloned child event composition.
CreativePartnerPane.tsx and InfoTooltip.tsx consume base tooltip primitives in feature contexts, while the same file also embeds feature-specific behavior.
This creates an ambiguous shared API surface where infrastructure and behavior policy are co-located.
findings.suggestion: Keep `shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` limited to primitive wrappers and move touch-specific behavior into a feature or behavior-layer module (e.g., `shared/components/behaviors/touchTooltip.tsx`).

findings.summary: Install scenario decision logic is coupled to a UI rendering registry type.
evidence: scenarios.ts imports `InstallVisualKey` from visuals.tsx, forcing logic-layer code to depend on a component-heavy file.
InstallInstructionsModal.tsx uses resolver output directly against `installVisualRegistry`, reinforcing the coupling between decision logic and presentation implementation.
findings.suggestion: Extract `InstallVisualKey` into a small shared contract file (e.g., `installInstructions/types.ts`) and keep scenarios/registry both depending on that contract instead of each other.

findings.summary: Media gallery controller layer mostly re-packages large context bags, making ownership and change boundaries unclear.
evidence: useMediaGalleryController accepts nested `shotContext`, `taskDetailsContext`, and `mobileContext`, then mostly forwards slices to other hooks.
useMediaGalleryHandlers takes multiple grouped objects with overlapping responsibilities (navigation, shot selection, task details mutation), indicating orchestration leakage.
ShotActions consumes a similarly dense grouped prop structure, extending cross-boundary parameter-bag patterns.
findings.suggestion: Define narrower domain services/hooks (shot navigation service, task-details opener, mobile tap behavior) with explicit small contracts, and compose them at the page/container boundary.

findings.summary: Dialog components expose inconsistent open-state APIs, increasing wrapper and adapter friction across the shared surface.
evidence: ConfirmDialog and InstallInstructionsModal use `open` + `(open:boolean)=>void` semantics.
DescriptionModal uses `isOpen` + `onClose`, then passes `onClose` into `Dialog` `onOpenChange`, conflating close action and state-change callback shape.
findings.suggestion: Standardize shared dialog-facing props to `open` and `onOpenChange(open:boolean)` across components; if close-only behavior is needed, adapt internally rather than changing public contracts.

findings.summary: Task orchestration handoff relies on multiple competing parameter key paths, signaling contract drift across producer/consumer boundaries.
evidence: Sibling lookup in `orchestrator.ts` queries three alternate run-id paths in one OR clause (`:191`) and three alternate orchestrator-id paths (`:208`).
Payload builders emit top-level ids plus nested orchestration contract (`payloadBuilder.ts:122-130`, `joinClips.ts:292-320`), so identity can be represented redundantly.
The need for fallback queries and mixed key locations indicates integration seams are not normalized at one boundary.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical orchestration identity schema (single run id field + single orchestrator id field, optionally mirrored only in `orchestration_contract`), enforce it in task creation helpers, and collapse completion queries to that schema with a migration/backfill path.

findings.summary: Video lightbox render hook and component depend on each other, creating a two-way seam that obscures ownership.
evidence: `VideoLightbox.tsx` imports and calls `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` (`:38`, `:100`).
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` imports `VideoLightboxProps` and model types from `../VideoLightbox` (`:8-12`).
This creates a cyclic type-level dependency and keeps hook API coupled to component-local declarations.
findings.suggestion: Move shared lightbox prop/model types into a dedicated `types` module under `MediaLightbox` and have both files depend on that module unidirectionally.

findings.summary: Data-access hooks close over module-level Supabase singletons, reducing explicit dependency handoffs and seam testability.
evidence: Each file initializes `const supabase = getSupabaseClient()` at module scope (`useTasks.ts:13`, `useGenerationMutations.ts:27`, `useDiscordSignIn.ts:5`, `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts:13`).
Consumers of these hooks cannot inject/override a client boundary in tests or alternate runtime flows.
This pattern repeats across unrelated modules, indicating a cross-seam coupling convention rather than isolated choice.
findings.suggestion: Adopt a shared `useSupabaseClient()` seam (or parameterized data layer helpers) so hooks resolve client dependencies at runtime and can be substituted in tests.

findings.summary: Button group prop construction is over-factored and memoized on unstable input, adding indirection without reducing churn.
evidence: `useButtonGroupProps` duplicates the same shared fields in four builders (`useButtonGroupProps.ts:56-132`) and memoizes output with dependency `[params]` (`:142`).
Caller creates a fresh nested `params` object literal each render (`useSharedLightboxState.ts:557-599`), so the memo boundary is effectively invalidated every render.
The wrapper chain also wraps simple pass-through callbacks (e.g., `handleUpscale` re-wrapped in `useSharedLightboxState.ts:580-582`).
findings.suggestion: Inline this construction in the calling hook (or memoize with granular dependencies), and keep only one small normalizer if needed for shared defaults.

findings.summary: Image edit state assembly injects fixed placeholder values that diverge from live behavior, reducing internal state honesty.
evidence: `buildRepositionAndPanelState` sets `isFlippedHorizontally: false` and `isSaving: false` unconditionally (`useImageEditValue.ts:217-218`).
Actual save/generation state is tracked through live flags in inline edit orchestration (e.g., `isSavingAsVariant` from reposition state in `useInlineEditState.ts:343`).
Returning static placeholders in the shared state object makes parts of the interface misleading to downstream consumers.
findings.suggestion: Either wire these fields to real source-of-truth state (if still needed) or remove/deprecate them from `ImageEditState` to avoid false signals.

findings.summary: Shot image editor callbacks add layered result/adaptor wrappers that obscure direct control flow.
evidence: Control flow fans out across `runCallback` (`:154-169`), three `handle*Result` wrappers (`:177-229`), then three adapter wrappers (`:231-247`) before usage.
The adapters collapse richer results back to booleans/simple objects, so much of the intermediate shape is transient.
Error reporting is centralized already, making multiple wrapper layers mostly structural overhead.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to direct callbacks with one shared `withHandledError` helper (or inline try/catch per action) and return final consumer shape directly.

findings.summary: Request contracts are built as untyped dictionaries and then force-cast across module boundaries.
evidence: `buildOrchestrationContract()` returns `Record<string, unknown>` instead of a concrete contract type.
`buildTravelRequestBodyV2()` returns `Record<string, unknown>`, and `generateVideo()` submits it via `createTravelBetweenImagesTask(requestBody as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskParams)`.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit DTO types for orchestration and travel request payloads, return those types from builders, and remove `unknown`/double-cast submission paths so compile-time checks enforce seam compatibility.

findings.summary: Cross-component API seams rely on spread-flattened prop bags that can silently override fields.
evidence: `resolveShotImagesEditorProps()` merges `displayOptions`, `imageState`, `editActions`, and `shotWorkflow` with plain spreads, so duplicate keys are last-write-wins with no warning.
`resolveJoinClipsSettingsFormProps()` similarly merges `clipSettings`, `motionConfig`, and `uiState` into one flattened object, increasing accidental coupling between independently owned groups.
findings.suggestion: Keep grouped props at render boundaries (nested objects) or introduce explicit namespaced resolved shapes with conflict checks to prevent accidental override semantics.

findings.summary: Edge operation modules mix typed result envelopes with thrown exceptions, creating inconsistent caller expectations.
evidence: `edgeOperation.ts` establishes `EdgeOperationResult` (`ok` union) as a shared failure/success contract.
`handleStorageOperations()` throws `new Error('Storage upload failed...')` on upload failures, while `handleThumbnail()` and `triggerCostCalculation()` return structured `edgeFailure(...)` results.
findings.suggestion: Standardize edge helpers on `EdgeOperationResult` end-to-end (including primary upload path) or standardize on throws with one adapter layer; avoid mixed styles in the same subsystem.

findings.summary: Required options in integration APIs are declared but ignored, making contracts misleading.
evidence: `InvalidationOptions.reason` and `VariantInvalidationOptions.reason` are required in type signatures but never read in invalidation execution paths.
`RateLimitConfig.identifierType` is defined and populated in `RATE_LIMITS`, but `checkRateLimit()` never uses it when composing keys or behavior.
findings.suggestion: Either consume these fields in logic/telemetry or make them optional/remove them; ensure exported option types reflect actual runtime behavior.

findings.summary: Shot-selection seams expose conflicting payload shapes between event bus and context state API.
evidence: Typed event `selectShotForAddition` requires `{ shotId, shotName }` in `AppCustomEvents`.
Context API `selectShotForAddition(shotId: string)` persists only ID and has no place for shotName, so same domain action carries different boundary contracts.
findings.suggestion: Unify the shot-selection contract across event and context layers (either ID-only everywhere or shared object shape) and codify one canonical payload type reused by both APIs.

findings.summary: Legacy compatibility fields are retained as first-class input without an enforced removal mechanism.
evidence: `legacyProjectImageSettings.ts:10-13` declares a remove-by date in comments, but the type still includes all deprecated fields (`:16-31`) and is merged into active input (`:38-40`).
`taskIdHelpers.ts:27-32` continues accepting legacy key names (`task_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`) with no deprecation gating, extending dual-path behavior.
findings.suggestion: Add a single normalization boundary that maps legacy keys to canonical fields, emit telemetry for legacy usage, and fail closed (or strip) legacy fields after the stated sunset date; then remove deprecated keys from public input types.

findings.summary: Deprecated aliases and stale parameters keep obsolete migration scaffolding in hot-path storage utilities.
evidence: `storage.ts:217-219` still exports deprecated `getStoragePublicUrl`, leaving two names for the same behavior.
`storage.ts:44` keeps `_isServiceRole` unused, and `generateThumbnail` keeps unused `_sourceBytes`, `_userId`, `_taskId` params (`:165-168`), indicating unresolved signature drift.
`taskConfig.ts:57-61` and `:105-108` preserve compatibility fallback flow without a bounded retirement path, contributing to lingering dual-mode internals.
findings.suggestion: Remove deprecated alias exports and unused parameters in one migration cut, then require canonical APIs only; for compatibility registries, add explicit expiry/version gates so fallback code can be deleted deterministically.

findings.summary: The delete-confirm hook mixes UI factory wrapping with unsafe casts, reducing clarity of internal control flow.
evidence: `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:48-58` returns a callback-created component tied to local state, adding indirection to a simple confirm-flow hook.
`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx:64` force-casts `deleteGenerationMutation.variables as string`, which can silently drift from mutation variable shape.
`supabaseTypeHelpers.ts:11-12` centralizes `as unknown as` casting, reinforcing a pattern of bypassing type guarantees at boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Return dialog props/state handlers from the hook instead of a component factory, and type `useDeleteGeneration` mutation variables explicitly so `deletingId` derives without casts.

findings.summary: Low-level helpers depend on brittle string heuristics that encode migration edge cases as scattered conditionals.
evidence: `preloader.ts:41-44` skips preloading via hardcoded filename substring (`_joined_frame.jpg`) rather than explicit media metadata/capability checks.
`imageAspectRatio.ts:57-59` only parses resolution strings containing lowercase `x` and directly splits/parses ad hoc, making behavior format-fragile.
findings.suggestion: Move these checks into shared typed predicates/parsers (e.g., `isPreloadableThumbnail`, `parseResolution`) with explicit accepted formats and unit tests, then replace inline string-branch logic.

findings.summary: Domain workflows are packaged under `shared/`, obscuring ownership boundaries.
evidence: `useAutoTopup` performs authenticated Supabase user reads and top-up mutation orchestration (`setup-auto-topup` edge function), which is billing-domain logic.
`useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator` manages generation queueing, thumbnail generation/upload, and generation query cache invalidation.
`ActiveLoRAsDisplay` and `AspectRatioSelector` encode LoRA/aspect-ratio domain semantics rather than generic presentation primitives.
findings.suggestion: Create domain-owned packages (for example `src/domains/billing/*` and `src/domains/generation/*`) and move these hooks/components there; keep `shared` for generic UI primitives and stateless utilities only.

findings.summary: Hook tests show stale module seams, indicating package boundaries have drifted without aligned ownership.
evidence: `useAutoTopup.ts` imports `getSupabaseClient` and `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, but its test mocks `supabase` and `@/shared/lib/errorHandler`.
`useAutoTopup.ts` imports `creditQueryKeys` from `@/shared/lib/queryKeys/credits`, while its test mocks `@/shared/lib/queryKeys`.
`useBackgroundThumbnailGenerator.ts` imports `@/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError`, but its test mocks `@/shared/lib/errorHandler`.
findings.suggestion: Co-locate tests with domain-owned modules after moving hooks, and align tests to stable public module entry points (barrel/API surface) instead of legacy internal paths.

findings.summary: Root configuration files combine unrelated ownership concerns, blurring directory and layer boundaries.
evidence: `tailwind.config.ts` scans both legacy and current directory shapes (`./pages`, `./components`, `./app`, `./src`), which suggests unresolved structural migration at the package level.
`vite.config.ts` includes environment/deployment-specific host allowlists inside build tooling config (`preview.allowedHosts`), coupling app package structure to deployment policy.
`vite.config.ts` also contains custom logging suppression and chunking policy in one root file, further centralizing mixed responsibilities.
findings.suggestion: Split root config ownership by concern: keep build mechanics in Vite config, move deploy-host policy to environment-driven config, and remove obsolete content globs once canonical source layout is finalized.

findings.summary: `useBatchImageLoading` is a feature-context adapter stored as a shared hook, flattening package boundaries.
evidence: The hook header explicitly states it is used by specific batch view components (`ShotBatchItemMobile`, `ShotBatchItemDesktop`), indicating feature-local intent.
It wires progressive image behavior with batch-view defaults (`priority: false`, `lazy: true`, `crossfadeMs: 200`) rather than exposing a domain-agnostic primitive.
findings.suggestion: Move this hook to the owning feature/package (for example a gallery or batch-view module) and keep only a lower-level reusable primitive in `shared/hooks`.

findings.summary: Error notifier governance contract in docs is stricter than the actual runtime behavior.
evidence: README.md states notifier is 'registered once at app startup' via `configureErrorNotifier(...)`.
handleError.ts permits `configureErrorNotifier(null)` (teardown) and subsequent non-null registration, and only throws when a different notifier is set while one is already registered.
findings.suggestion: Align contract and runtime by either documenting the supported reconfiguration lifecycle explicitly or tightening `configureErrorNotifier` to enforce a true one-time bootstrap-only registration path.

findings.summary: Supabase access policy is documented but not technically enforced at the boundary where the contract is declared.
evidence: README.md declares new code should avoid legacy proxy imports and use `getSupabaseClient()`.
client.ts provides canonical access but includes no enforcement mechanism (lint guard, restricted export pattern, or runtime warning) to prevent policy drift elsewhere.
findings.suggestion: Add an enforceable governance mechanism (e.g., lint `no-restricted-imports` for legacy proxy paths and/or a compatibility wrapper with deprecation warnings) so policy is machine-checked rather than docs-only.

findings.summary: A major shared UI primitive carries undocumented compatibility semantics that function as an architectural contract.
evidence: button.tsx merges core and retro APIs in one surface (`ButtonVariant`/`ButtonSize` unions) and silently maps retro usage to core defaults (`ghost`/`default`) before applying `retroButtonPolicy`.
README runtime contracts section does not define compatibility/versioning expectations for this widely shared boundary.
findings.suggestion: Document and formalize button compatibility policy (supported variants, migration guarantees, deprecation path) and consider splitting legacy/retro behavior behind explicit adapters to reduce hidden cross-era coupling.

findings.summary: Multiple wrapper hooks add ceremony but little domain behavior.
evidence: useMediaGalleryController primarily re-groups arguments and forwards results from two other hooks.
useButtonGroupProps duplicates shared fields into four prop objects using near-identical helper builders.
useInlineEditState splits orchestration into many wrappers that mostly pass through fields between hook outputs.
findings.suggestion: Collapse pass-through wrappers into fewer composition points: keep only boundaries that add policy/decision logic, and move pure object-shaping helpers next to their single consumer components to reduce abstraction surface.

findings.summary: Object-bag chaining and ineffective memo dependencies increase indirection without reducing work.
evidence: useButtonGroupProps memo depends on `[params]`, so any new params object invalidates memo while still paying abstraction cost.
useVideoLightboxRenderModel introduces multiple intermediate memoized structures before final state assembly, each with long dependency sets.
MediaGallery passes large grouped objects across hook seams, increasing conversion and tracing overhead.
findings.suggestion: Prefer direct prop passing at call sites for single-use structures, replace `[params]` with stable scalar dependencies (or remove memo), and merge adjacent render-model layers where they do not cut real recomputation or API complexity.

findings.summary: The orchestration interface is named as a contract but implemented as drifting loose shapes.
evidence: buildOrchestrationContract returns `Record<string, unknown>` and accepts broad string inputs instead of a typed discriminated contract.
individualTravelSegment writes orchestration data in multiple channels (top-level params, nested details, contract object) and strips legacy keys to compensate.
orchestrator completion queries multiple legacy/alternate key names to find the same identifiers.
findings.suggestion: Define one typed orchestration schema (shared between app and edge functions), emit identifiers only in that schema, and migrate consumers to read only canonical keys with a temporary versioned adapter for legacy tasks.

findings.summary: Related modules use different post-error behavior (rethrow vs swallow) for similar failures.
evidence: Task creation modules catch, report, and rethrow to preserve failure semantics.
useDiscordSignIn reports errors but always suppresses them, leaving callers without explicit failure signaling.
MediaGallery handlers mix silent early returns after handleError with try/catch paths, yielding inconsistent caller expectations.
findings.suggestion: Document and enforce per-layer error contracts (e.g., async actions return Result or throw consistently); update UI hooks to either return explicit failure states or rethrow after reporting, rather than mixing silent suppression and propagation.
